title: Arbitova Verification Spec v1
description: Schema for the verificationURI that a buyer attaches to an escrow. Seller reads the spec to know what 'delivered' means. Arbiter reads it during disputes. Optional: a programmatic verifier endpoint can be declared so buyer agents evaluate delivery automatically.

version.description: Schema version identifier. Must be literally 'arbitova-spec-v1'.

mode.description: manual = human reads the prose acceptance. programmatic = a verifier endpoint can judge delivery automatically.

buyer.description: Buyer Ethereum address. Optional — informational only.

seller.description: Seller Ethereum address. Optional — informational only.

amount.description: Decimal USDC amount agreed out of band. Informational.

description.description: Prose criteria the seller must meet. Read by humans (buyer, seller, arbiter).

outOfScope.description: Things the seller is explicitly NOT responsible for.

check.description: Only present when mode=programmatic. Defines how to automatically verify a delivery.

endpoint.description: HTTPS URL the buyer's verifier POSTs { escrowId, deliveryPayloadURI } to. Response: { pass: bool, reasons: string[], evidence?: string }.

authScheme.description: How the verifier authenticates the incoming check request.

dispute.description: Optional hints to the arbiter about how to resolve disputes on this spec.

tieBreak.description: Default arbiter action if evidence is inconclusive. Non-binding guidance.

evidenceURIs.description: Links to supporting docs the arbiter should consult.
